task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios is currently reported as ON TRACK with minor risks, but several schedule-sensitive issues require close management before the July 25 Meridian product demo and August UAT window. Core platform work is progressing: the API gateway is complete, data ingestion is 85% complete, and the client dashboard is 60% complete. However, integration testing is only 40% complete, multiple Q2 follow-on items are overdue, and two high-severity ingestion defects remain open.

The most important leadership concern is that the July delivery path is tightly coupled to external Meridian dependencies and unresolved technical risks. UAT has already been extended by one week, and the Q3 roadmap notes that UAT slippage of more than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger a Section 7.1 penalty review.

Current Progress
1. Milestone Status
- Overall project status: ON TRACK, with minor risks.
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion date is 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard: 60% complete; target completion date is 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off target is 2026-07-25.

2. Q2 Deliverables Carryover
Completed:
- Requirements documentation v1.0.
- System architecture design.
- API gateway implementation.
- Development environment setup.
- Data schema finalization.

Still in progress or overdue:
- Data ingestion module: in progress, target 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: in progress, target 2026-07-22.
- Integration test suite setup: overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning: overdue from 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access.
- Performance benchmarking report: overdue from 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor.

3. Sprint 14 Testing Status
- Authentication flows: 4/4 passed.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 passed, 2 failed.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete; chart rendering and 100k-record load benchmark remain incomplete or not run.
- Overall Sprint 14 QA status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 failed, 3 not yet run.

Open high-severity defects:
- HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format, affecting approximately 40% of Meridian source data. Blocked pending clean Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15.
- HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion over approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; pipeline crashes around the 210k record mark. Root cause is under investigation.

Key Risks
1. Meridian Source Data Quality Issues
- Risk ID: R-01.
- Likelihood: High.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Active.
- Current manifestation: inconsistent date formats are blocking completion of the data ingestion module and affect about 40% of source data.
- Mitigation owner: Amir Hassan.
- Current dependency: Meridian is expected to provide a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.

2. GeoSync License Renewal
- Risk ID: R-02.
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending legal review.
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14.
- Demo impact: the July 25 demo depends on resolving this before the demo.

3. Dashboard and Pipeline Performance
- Risk ID: R-03.
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: dashboard load-time benchmark for 100k records has not been run; performance benchmarking report is overdue.
- Additional concern: ingestion of files larger than approximately 200k records currently causes a memory overflow, which threatens the Q3 target of 500k records/min sustained throughput.
- Owner: Derek Okafor for benchmarking; Amir Hassan for ingestion defect root-cause investigation.

4. UAT Timeline Slippage
- Risk ID: R-04.
- Likelihood: High.
- Impact: Medium.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: a one-week UAT extension has already been consumed. UAT is now scheduled for August 17-28.
- Strategic impact: slippage greater than two weeks may push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

5. Meridian IT Environment Access
- Risk ID: R-06.
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: Medium.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: UAT environment provisioning remains blocked by Meridian IT access and the pending IT infrastructure diagram.
- Meridian due date: infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT access expected by 2026-07-19.

6. Key Person Dependency
- Risk ID: R-05.
- Likelihood: Low.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Monitored.
- Current issue: no backup technical lead has been identified for Derek Okafor, who owns or is closely involved in several critical workstreams, including performance benchmarking and license/demo readiness.

Upcoming Q3 Commitments
1. Complete System Integration — July
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.

2. Product Demo for Meridian — 2026-07-25
Confirmed in the July 8 Meridian sync. Demo commitments include:
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data.
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.

Critical demo prerequisites:
- Meridian clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15.
- GeoSync license resolution due 2026-07-14.
- Draft demo agenda to Meridian due 2026-07-18.
- Benchmark dashboard load times before demo.
- Resolve or appropriately contain high-severity ingestion issues before live demonstration.

3. User Acceptance Testing — August 17-28
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted.
- Meridian IT has indicated UAT environment access will be provisioned by 2026-07-19.

4. Performance Hardening — August to September
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput target: 500k records/min sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still to be determined.

5. Launch Preparation — September
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization using a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01.

6. Proposed Q3 Feature Additions Requiring Change Control
- Scheduled ingestion jobs using cron-based triggers.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor deferred from Q2.

Issues Requiring Leadership Attention
1. Ensure GeoSync License Resolution by 2026-07-14
Leadership may need to ensure Legal and project teams close the GeoSync renewal on time. A delay could directly affect the July 25 demo.

2. Escalate Meridian Data and Environment Dependencies if Deadlines Slip
Two Meridian dependencies are critical: clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 and infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12, followed by UAT access by 2026-07-19. Leadership should be prepared to escalate with Meridian sponsorship if either dependency slips.

3. Treat Performance Benchmarking as a Demo-Critical Deliverable
The benchmarking report is already overdue, and large-file ingestion currently fails around 210k records. Leadership should request a clear recovery plan, including target fix date, contingency plan for the demo, and ownership for dashboard and ingestion performance validation.

4. Review UAT Schedule Risk and Contract Exposure
The Q3 roadmap states that UAT slippage of more than two weeks could move launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review. Leadership should monitor UAT readiness weekly through July and August and confirm whether contractual exposure mitigation is needed.

5. Reduce Key-Person Dependency on Technical Lead
Derek Okafor is associated with several critical delivery items, including benchmarking and demo readiness, and there is no identified backup technical lead. Leadership should consider assigning a backup or deputy lead through the July demo and UAT period.

6. Enforce Change Control for Proposed Q3 Additions
The proposed Q3 feature additions should remain subject to change control. Leadership should avoid committing to added scope until integration, demo, UAT readiness, and performance hardening are stabilized.

Recommended Leadership Watchlist
- By 2026-07-12: Confirm receipt of Meridian IT infrastructure diagram or escalate.
- By 2026-07-14: Confirm GeoSync license renewal is complete.
- By 2026-07-15: Confirm receipt and usability of Meridian clean sample dataset.
- By 2026-07-18: Confirm data ingestion completion and demo agenda delivery.
- By 2026-07-20: Confirm integration test suite setup is complete.
- By 2026-07-22: Confirm client dashboard v1 completion and dashboard benchmark status.
- By 2026-07-25: Confirm integration test sign-off and readiness for live-data Meridian demo.
- By 2026-07-19 to 2026-08-17: Confirm UAT environment access and full UAT readiness.

Bottom Line
Project Helios remains broadly on track, but the margin for delay is narrowing. The July 25 demo and August UAT window are the key near-term success gates. Leadership attention should focus on external dependency escalation, license closure, high-severity ingestion defects, overdue performance validation, and protecting the team from additional scope until core Q3 commitments are secured.
